creator: barancezayirli.com

evidence.text: The knowledge base grows organically because curation happens at the natural inflection points of engineering work, not as a separate documentation sprint. A sprint retrospective produces a knowledge entry when the team learned something that will change how they work. A postmortem produces an entry when the root cause reveals a constraint or assumption that applies beyond the incident. An architecture review produces an ADR that is not just a record of the decision but a record of the rejected alternatives and the reasoning behind the choice.
